text: Continuous integration is a development practice where team members integrate their code changes frequently, usually several times a day. Each integration is verified through automated builds and by running tests, which helps teams catch issues early. This process uses CI pipelines, which are automated sequences that build the code, run tests, and report results. By finding errors soon after they occur, developers can fix them quickly and prevent bigger problems later. Continuous integration makes software development more reliable and efficient, as it reduces manual work and ensures that the codebase stays stable.
